name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 60 Monday. SMH Friday $506.44 (+5.1% 1D, +26.9% 1M, +27.0% 3M) — 16-day SOX consecutive-up streak — extending into Asian Monday LIVE: KOSPI +2.2%, Taiwan TAIEX +2.7%, Nikkei +1.4%. Chip complex structurally decoupled from crisis (~5% crisis attribution). Branch 3 (Brent gap to $100.47) reinforces structural-decoupling thesis. Drivers: TSMC Q1 + capex +32% to $54B for 2026; 16-day SOX streak with record SOXX/SMH inflows ($5.5B); Taiwan single-stock fund-limit deregulation; Samsung Q1 profit surge + helium-cascade falsification (Phil Kornbluth Day 4 framework holding via SK Hynix 'diversified suppliers + sufficient inventory' + Samsung Q1 helium reuse 4.7 tons/year + 19% reuse rate). Samsung April 30 earnings is next binary close.

current.april_inflows: SOXX/SMH combined $5.5B; SMH alone $3.4B (all-time fund inflow record)
current.sox_streak: 16 consecutive up sessions (+38.7% in ~4 weeks) — pace for biggest monthly gain since Feb 2000

price_snapshots.trigger: Promoted from edge-only to node per markets.md F2; first formal price snapshot. TSMC fund-limit deregulation (JPM $6B+ inflow estimate) + TSMC capex +32% to $54B + 16-day SOX streak + record April inflows + SK Hynix Q1 72% margin + Samsung Q1 8x YoY operating profit guidance + Samsung helium reuse system disclosure.
price_snapshots.delta_context: 16-day SOX streak is the longest momentum-driven rally of 2026; pace for biggest monthly gain since Feb 2000 (the dot-com top). Editor must not attribute meaningful portion of Friday's chip rally to crisis dynamics — rally is regulatory/earnings/structural. Samsung April 30 earnings is the next binary catalyst.

signals.content: SMH $506.44 (+5.1% 1D) — THE FRIDAY MARKET HEADLINE YESTERDAY MISSED [CONFIRMED CORRECTION]. Yesterday's dossier had SMH at $481.85 (+1.1%) — wrong by $25/share and 4 percentage points. Per Benzinga April 24, the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has now gained for 16 consecutive trading sessions, +38.7% in roughly four weeks — pace for the biggest monthly gain since February 2000. April fund inflows at record: SOXX/SMH combined $5.5B (already exceeding any previous full-month inflow record); SMH alone $3.4B (all-time fund inflow record). Primary chain (~95% structural, ~5% crisis): Taiwan Financial Supervisory Commission single-stock fund-limit raised 10% → 25% (JPM $6B+ inflow estimate) → TSMC ADR rally → SMH top-holding lift; TSMC announces 32% capex hike to $54B for 2026 → multi-year structural AI demand confirmation; SK Hynix Q1 72% operating margin (above Nvidia's 65%) → AI memory pricing power confirmed; SMH 16-day consecutive up streak = longest momentum-driven rally of 2026; record April inflows = systematic+passive money chasing the move. Crisis-linked sub-chain (5%): helium-cascade falsification at SK Hynix + Samsung tier-1 level removes a tail risk that had been overhanging; 'war doesn't break chips' narrative now operationally proven. The fact that SMH +5.1% Friday occurred ALONGSIDE three carriers in CENTCOM, new Trump destroy-mine-laying-boats ROE, Mojtaba's first attributed statement in 42 days, and Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan dispatch — and the chip complex moved purely on TSMC capex + Taiwan regulatory + SK Hynix earnings — is the empirical demonstration that the crisis is no longer the dominant equity-market driver for the AI-semiconductor cluster. (Sources: yfinance Saturday script; Benzinga April 24; TSMC investor release; Samsung Global Newsroom; CNBC; SK Hynix earnings)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED corrected market data + structural-rally attribution at multi-source level.

signals.content: SAMSUNG Q1 GUIDANCE 8x YoY OPERATING PROFIT — HELIUM REUSE SYSTEM DEPLOYED [CONFIRMED]. Samsung Electronics issued Q1 2026 earnings guidance: operating profit ₩57.2 trillion (~$37.8 billion), 8x YoY gain and all-time quarterly high; revenue ₩133 trillion (~$88.6 billion), +68% YoY. Memory-chip demand from AI compute is the structural driver; HBM4 (11.7Gbps) entering production this quarter. Samsung publicly disclosed deployment of helium reuse system reducing annual consumption by ~4.7 tons with ~19% reuse rate. Combined with SK Hynix Q1 disclosure (diversified suppliers + sufficient inventory), the tier-1 chipmaker pair has BOTH publicly confirmed helium hedging that prevents margin pass-through from the +40% spot premium. Full earnings release April 30 (date corrected from April 29 in yesterday's brief) is the binary test for full helium cascade falsification at tier-1 level. Industrial-gas-producer rotation (Linde, BASF) still operationally valid because they capture the spot premium without margin offset. (Sources: Samsung Global Newsroom April 7 guidance release; qz.com; SamMobile; TipRanks; Shacknews; SammyGuru)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED via Samsung Global Newsroom primary + multi-source relay.

signals.content: Asian Monday session chip-led rally extends Branch 3 confirmation [CONFIRMED]. KOSPI +2.2%, Taiwan TAIEX +2.7%, Nikkei +1.4% — three-way Asian chip rally Monday morning. SMH Friday +5.1% with 16-day SOX consecutive-up streak; record SOXX/SMH inflows ($5.5B). Chip complex ~5% crisis attribution; structural AI capex thesis (TSMC capex +32% to $54B) intact. Helium-cascade Phil Kornbluth Day-4 falsification framework holding at tier-1 customer level despite spot +40-100% — industrial-gas producers (Linde, BASF) capture spot premium without chip-margin pass-through. Samsung April 30 binary close. (Sources: yfinance, Trading Economics, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware)

edges.context: Day 58 Saturday. Taiwan Financial Supervisory Commission single-stock fund-limit raised 10% → 25% (JPM $6B+ inflow). TSMC is ~40% of Taiwanese market cap; every passive and active fund must rebalance. TSMC capex +32% to $54B for 2026 confirms multi-year structural AI demand. TAIEX +3.2% Friday on regulatory catalyst. Direct first-order driver of SMH 16-day streak.

Day 58 Saturday. KOSPI +18.6% 1M / +30.8% 3M on SK Hynix Q1 72% operating margin (above Nvidia 65%) + Samsung Q1 guidance ₩57.2T operating profit 8x YoY + Samsung HBM4 entering production + helium reuse system disclosure. The Korean tier-1 chipmaker pair structurally drives the AI-memory leg of the chip complex.

Day 58 Saturday. Helium-cascade falsification confirmed at BOTH Korean tier-1 chipmakers: SK Hynix Q1 (diversified suppliers + sufficient inventory + limited production impact) + Samsung Q1 (helium reuse system, ~4.7 tons/yr reduction, ~19% reuse rate). The 'war doesn't break chips' narrative is now operationally proven at tier-1 customer level. Industrial-gas-producer rotation (Linde, BASF) still valid because producers capture spot premium without margin offset.

Day 58 Saturday. SMH 16-day SOX consecutive-up streak +38.7% in ~4 weeks — pace for biggest monthly gain since Feb 2000. Record $5.5B April SOXX/SMH inflows. Chip complex is now ~7% of S&P 500 weight; passively bid via index flows. Structural AI-driven rally has decoupled from crisis narrative.

Day 58 Saturday. SOXX/SMH April inflows record $5.5B. SMH +27.0% 3M return is the cleanest non-crisis structural-growth window. AI structural-growth thesis attracting US capital independent of geopolitical regime. TSMC US fab buildout ($165B+ capex) is the parallel reshoring leg.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung April 30 earnings call flags helium/supply as Q2 guidance risk
trigger_points.mechanism: If Samsung's earnings call and outlook commentary flag helium as a guidance risk for Q2, the helium → semiconductor cascade re-opens at tier-1 level and the 16-day SOX streak breaks. SMH would re-price -3 to -5% on the morning. If Samsung confirms similar margin pricing power and helium-reuse-system absorbs the supply tightness without guidance impact, the streak extends and the cascade is fully falsified at tier-1 level. Falsifiable: April 30 8:30 AM KST.

trigger_points.condition: SOX 16-day consecutive-up streak breaks (any single down session)
trigger_points.mechanism: Pace for biggest monthly gain since Feb 2000 (the dot-com top). The streak's length itself is a momentum-extension signal; any single down session would mark the inflection. Falsifiable: weekly SOX close.
